title: Execution Isolation
summary: A lesson on separating sensitive execution contexts so that workloads remain legible to operators without exposing the underlying private data.

bodySections.title: Why Isolation Exists
bodySections.body:
Isolation keeps unrelated work from leaking into one another's execution paths. In a confidential system, that matters for both correctness and privacy.

You want operators to manage infrastructure, but you do not want infrastructure operators to gain unnecessary visibility into sensitive user inputs.

bodySections.title: Operational Benefit
bodySections.body:
Good isolation is not only defensive. It also makes reasoning about workloads easier, because boundaries are explicit and failure domains are easier to describe.

That kind of clarity is what turns privacy from an abstract promise into a system property.

quizQuestions.prompt: Why is isolation useful in confidential execution?
What balance does the lesson describe?
choices: Operators manage infrastructure without seeing unnecessary private data
Operators see all private data to optimize performance
Operators are removed from the system entirely
